flashcards.front: What is the main difference between Map and Collection interfaces?
flashcards.back: Map stores key-value pairs while Collection stores single elements. Map cannot have duplicate keys.

flashcards.front: Name the three collection views provided by the Map interface.
What is the time complexity of basic operations in HashMap?
flashcards.back: O(1) for put(), get(), and remove() operations under ideal conditions.

flashcards.front: When would you use TreeMap over HashMap?
flashcards.back: When you need the elements to be sorted by keys, either naturally or using a custom comparator.
It returns a comparator that considers null values to be less than non-null values, placing them first in sorted order.
